  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Exploring Human Health Data with ggplot2 and dplyr

Alyssah Mack

2026-06-10

Introduction

In this exercise you will work with a dataset of 200 human samples containing physical measurements and clinical lab values. You will practice:

  • Reading tabular data into R
  • Manipulating data with dplyr (filter, select, mutate, group_by, summarize)
  • Visualizing data with ggplot2

The dataset (human_health_data.tsv) contains the following columns:

Column Description
sample_id Unique sample identifier
sex Biological sex (Male / Female)
height_cm Height in centimetres
weight_kg Weight in kilograms
hair_color Hair color
eye_color Eye color
blood_glucose_mgdl Fasting blood glucose (mg/dL)
LDL_mgdl LDL cholesterol (mg/dL)

Part 1 — Loading the data

1.1 Load the tidyverse package and read human_health_data.tsv into a tibble called health. Use read_tsv(). Print the first few rows with head().

library(tidyverse)

health <- read_tsv("human_health_data.tsv")

head(health)

1.2 Use glimpse() to get an overview of the data types in health.

glimpse(health)

Part 2 — Selecting and filtering (select, filter)

2.1 Create a new tibble called clinical that contains only the columns sample_id, sex, blood_glucose_mgdl, and LDL_mgdl.

health <- as_tibble(health)

# Create a tibble called clinical with only the specified columns
clinical <- health |> select(sample_id, sex, blood_glucose_mgdl, LDL_mgdl)

2.2 From the original health tibble, filter to keep only Female samples whose blood_glucose_mgdl is greater than 100 mg/dL (pre-diabetic range). How many samples meet this criterion?

femaleHighBloodGlucose <- filter(health, sex=="Female", blood_glucose_mgdl > 100)

# 35 samples meet this criteria

2.3 Filter health to keep only samples with brown hair or blue eyes. Store the result as brown_or_blue.

brown_or_blue <- filter(health, hair_color=="Brown"|eye_color=="Blue")

Part 3 — Creating new variables (mutate)

3.1 Add a column called bmi (Body Mass Index) to health using the formula:

\[BMI = \frac{weight\_kg}{(height\_cm / 100)^2}\]

Round to two decimal places. Save the result back to health.

bmi <- health$weight_kg/(health$height_cm/100)^2
health <- health |> mutate(bmi)

3.2 Add a column called glucose_category that classifies each sample:

  • "Normal" if blood_glucose_mgdl < 100
  • "Pre-diabetic" if between 100 and 125 (inclusive)
  • "Diabetic" if blood_glucose_mgdl > 125

Hint: Use case_when() inside mutate().

health <- health |> mutate(glucose_category =  
      case_when(blood_glucose_mgdl < 100 ~ "Normal",
      blood_glucose_mgdl >= 100 & blood_glucose_mgdl <= 125 ~ "Pre-Diabetic",
                blood_glucose_mgdl>125 ~ "Diabetic"))

Part 4 — Summarizing by group (group_by, summarize)

4.1 Calculate the mean and standard deviation of bmi separately for Males and Females. Your output should have three columns: sex, mean_bmi, and sd_bmi.

health |> group_by(sex) |> summarise(
  mean_bmi = mean(bmi),
    sd_bmi = sd(bmi)
)

4.2 Count how many samples fall into each glucose_category, broken down by sex. Your output should have columns sex, glucose_category, and n.

health |> group_by(sex, glucose_category) |> summarise(
    n = n())

4.3 For each hair_color, calculate the mean LDL_mgdl. Arrange the result from highest to lowest mean LDL.

health |> group_by(hair_color) |> summarise(
    mean_LDL = mean(LDL_mgdl),
) |> arrange(desc(mean_LDL))

Part 5 — Visualization with ggplot2

For each plot, make sure to add informative axis labels and a title using labs().

5.1 Histogram — distribution of blood glucose

Plot a histogram of blood_glucose_mgdl. Color the bars by sex using fill. Use alpha = 0.6 so overlapping bars are visible. Set appropriate binwidth.


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuZ2dwbG90KGhlYWx0aCxcbiAgICAgICBtYXBwaW5nID0gYWVzKHg9Ymxvb2RfZ2x1Y29zZV9tZ2RsKSkgK1xuICAgICAgIGdlb21faGlzdG9ncmFtKGFlcyhjb2xvdXI9c2V4KSwgYWxwaGE9MC42KSArXG4gICAgICAgbGFicyh4PVwiQmxvb2QgR2x1Y29zZSAobWcvZEwpXCIsIHk9XCJDb3VudFwiLCB0aXRsZT1cIk1hbGUgYW5kIEZlbWFsZSBCbG9vZCBHbHVjb3NlXCIpXG5gYGAifQ== -->

```r
ggplot(health,
       mapping = aes(x=blood_glucose_mgdl)) +
       geom_histogram(aes(colour=sex), alpha=0.6) +
       labs(x=\Blood Glucose (mg/dL)\, y=\Count\, title=\Male and Female Blood Glucose\)
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->
```r
ggplot(health,
       mapping = aes(x=blood_glucose_mgdl)) +
       geom_histogram(aes(colour=sex), alpha=0.6) +
       labs(x=\Blood Glucose (mg/dL)\, y=\Count\, title=\Male and Female Blood Glucose\)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->

EAAAQJ09gEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEykDg/wNJ5uZUENA04wAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==" <!-- rnb-plot-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


**5.2 Scatter plot — height vs. weight**

Create a scatter plot of `height_cm` (x-axis) vs. `weight_kg` (y-axis),
coloring points by `sex`. Add a linear regression line for each sex using
`geom_smooth(method = "lm")`.


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVaMmR3Ykc5MEtHaGxZV3gwYUN4Y2JpQWdJQ0FnSUNCdFlYQndhVzVuSUQwZ1lXVnpLSGc5YUdWcFoyaDBYMk50TENCNVBYZGxhV2RvZEY5clp5a3BJQ3RjYmlBZ0lDQWdJQ0JuWlc5dFgzQnZhVzUwS0dGbGN5aGpiMnh2Y2oxelpYZ3BLU0FyWEc0Z0lDQWdJQ0FnWjJWdmJWOXpiVzl2ZEdnb2JXVjBhRzlrUFZ3aWJHMWNJaWtnSzF4dUlDQWdJQ0FnSUd4aFluTW9lRDFjSWtobGFXZG9kQ0FvWTIwcFhDSXNJSGs5WENKWFpXbG5hSFFnS0d0bktWd2lMQ0IwYVhSc1pUMGdYQ0pTWld4aGRHbHZibk5vYVhBZ1ltVjBkMlZsYmlCSVpXbG5hSFFnWVc1a0lGZGxhV2RvZEZ3aUtWeHVYRzVnWUdBaWZRPT0gLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmdncGxvdChoZWFsdGgsXG4gICAgICAgbWFwcGluZyA9IGFlcyh4PWhlaWdodF9jbSwgeT13ZWlnaHRfa2cpKSArXG4gICAgICAgZ2VvbV9wb2ludChhZXMoY29sb3I9c2V4KSkgK1xuICAgICAgIGdlb21fc21vb3RoKG1ldGhvZD1cXGxtXFwpICtcbiAgICAgICBsYWJzKHg9XFxIZWlnaHQgKGNtKVxcLCB5PVxcV2VpZ2h0IChrZylcXCwgdGl0bGU9IFxcUmVsYXRpb25zaGlwIGJldHdlZW4gSGVpZ2h0IGFuZCBXZWlnaHRcXClcblxuYGBgXG5cbjwhLS0gcm5iLXNvdXJjZS1lbmQgLS0+XG4ifQ== -->

ggplot(health,
       mapping = aes(x=height_cm, y=weight_kg)) +
       geom_point(aes(color=sex)) +
       geom_smooth(method=\lm\) +
       labs(x=\Height (cm)\, y=\Weight (kg)\, title= \Relationship between Height and Weight\)




<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dVoyZHdiRzkwS0dobFlXeDBhQ3hjYmlBZ0lDQWdJQ0J0WVhCd2FXNW5JRDBnWVdWektIZzlhR1ZwWjJoMFgyTnRMQ0I1UFhkbGFXZG9kRjlyWnlrcElDdGNiaUFnSUNBZ0lDQm5aVzl0WDNCdmFXNTBLR0ZsY3loamIyeHZjajF6WlhncEtTQXJYRzRnSUNBZ0lDQWdaMlZ2YlY5emJXOXZkR2dvYldWMGFHOWtQVnhjYkcxY1hDa2dLMXh1SUNBZ0lDQWdJR3hoWW5Nb2VEMWNYRWhsYVdkb2RDQW9ZMjBwWEZ3c0lIazlYRnhYWldsbmFIUWdLR3RuS1Z4Y0xDQjBhWFJzWlQwZ1hGeFNaV3hoZEdsdmJuTm9hWEFnWW1WMGQyVmxiaUJJWldsbmFIUWdZVzVrSUZkbGFXZG9kRnhjS1Z4dVhHNWdZR0JjYm1CZ1lDSjkgLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmBgYHJcbmdncGxvdChoZWFsdGgsXG4gICAgICAgbWFwcGluZyA9IGFlcyh4PWhlaWdodF9jbSwgeT13ZWlnaHRfa2cpKSArXG4gICAgICAgZ2VvbV9wb2ludChhZXMoY29sb3I9c2V4KSkgK1xuICAgICAgIGdlb21fc21vb3RoKG1ldGhvZD1cXGxtXFwpICtcbiAgICAgICBsYWJzKHg9XFxIZWlnaHQgKGNtKVxcLCB5PVxcV2VpZ2h0IChrZylcXCwgdGl0bGU9IFxcUmVsYXRpb25zaGlwIGJldHdlZW4gSGVpZ2h0IGFuZCBXZWlnaHRcXClcblxuYGBgXG5gYGBcblxuPCEtLSBybmItc291cmNlLWVuZCAtLT5cbiJ9 -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuZ2dwbG90KGhlYWx0aCxcbiAgICAgICBtYXBwaW5nID0gYWVzKHg9aGVpZ2h0X2NtLCB5PXdlaWdodF9rZykpICtcbiAgICAgICBnZW9tX3BvaW50KGFlcyhjb2xvcj1zZXgpKSArXG4gICAgICAgZ2VvbV9zbW9vdGgobWV0aG9kPVxcbG1cXCkgK1xuICAgICAgIGxhYnMoeD1cXEhlaWdodCAoY20pXFwsIHk9XFxXZWlnaHQgKGtnKVxcLCB0aXRsZT0gXFxSZWxhdGlvbnNoaXAgYmV0d2VlbiBIZWlnaHQgYW5kIFdlaWdodFxcKVxuXG5gYGBcbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
```r
ggplot(health,
       mapping = aes(x=height_cm, y=weight_kg)) +
       geom_point(aes(color=sex)) +
       geom_smooth(method=\lm\) +
       labs(x=\Height (cm)\, y=\Weight (kg)\, title= \Relationship between Height and Weight\)

```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1wbG90LWJlZ2luIGV5Sm9aV2xuYUhRaU9qTXdPUzR3TWpNMUxDSjNhV1IwYUNJNk5UQXdMQ0prY0draU9pMHhMQ0p6YVhwbFgySmxhR0YyYVc5eUlqb3dMQ0pqYjI1a2FYUnBiMjV6SWpwYlhYMD0gLS0+XG5cbjxpbWcgc3JjPVxcZGF0YTppbWFnZS9wbmc7YmFzZTY0XG4ifQ== -->

EAAAhCAAAQgAAEIQAACECgSAijoRfKg6SYEIAABCEAAAhCAAAQgAAEI5DYBFPTcfj60DgIQgAAEIAABCEAAAhCAAASKhAAKepE8aLoJAQhAAAIQgAAEIAABCEAAArlNAAU9t58PrYMABCAAAQhAAAIQgAAEIACBIiGAgl4kD5puQgACEIAABCAAAQhAAAIQgEBuE0BBz+3nQ+sgAAEIQAACEIAABCAAAQhAoEgIoKAXyYOmmxCAAAQgAAEIQAACEIAABCCQ2wT+P6xps8PrJ2RBAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC" />

<!-- rnb-plot-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


**5.3 Box plot — BMI by sex**

Make a box plot of `bmi` for each `sex`. Overlay the individual data points
using `geom_jitter()` with `width = 0.2` and `alpha = 0.4`.


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVaMmR3Ykc5MEtHaGxZV3gwYUN4Y2JpQWdJQ0FnSUNCdFlYQndhVzVuSUQwZ1lXVnpLSGc5YzJWNExDQjVQV0p0YVNrcElDdGNiaUFnSUNBZ0lDQm5aVzl0WDJKdmVIQnNiM1FvWVd4d2FHRTlNQzQwS1NBclhHNGdJQ0FnSUNBZ1oyVnZiVjlxYVhSMFpYSW9kMmxrZEdnOU1DNHlLU0FyWEc0Z0lDQWdJQ0FnYkdGaWN5aDRQVndpVTJWNFhDSXNJSGs5WENKQ1RVa2dLR3RuTDIxZU1pbGNJaXdnZEdsMGJHVTlYQ0pOWVd4bElHRnVaQ0JHWlcxaGJHVWdRazFKWENJcFhHNWdZR0FpZlE9PSAtLT5cblxuYGBgclxuZ2dwbG90KGhlYWx0aCxcbiAgICAgICBtYXBwaW5nID0gYWVzKHg9c2V4LCB5PWJtaSkpICtcbiAgICAgICBnZW9tX2JveHBsb3QoYWxwaGE9MC40KSArXG4gICAgICAgZ2VvbV9qaXR0ZXIod2lkdGg9MC4yKSArXG4gICAgICAgbGFicyh4PVxcU2V4XFwsIHk9XFxCTUkgKGtnL21eMilcXCwgdGl0bGU9XFxNYWxlIGFuZCBGZW1hbGUgQk1JXFwpXG5gYGBcblxuPCEtLSBybmItc291cmNlLWVuZCAtLT5cbiJ9 -->

ggplot(health,
       mapping = aes(x=sex, y=bmi)) +
       geom_boxplot(alpha=0.4) +
       geom_jitter(width=0.2) +
       labs(x=\Sex\, y=\BMI (kg/m^2)\, title=\Male and Female BMI\)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuZ2dwbG90KGhlYWx0aCxcbiAgICAgICBtYXBwaW5nID0gYWVzKHg9c2V4LCB5PWJtaSkpICtcbiAgICAgICBnZW9tX2JveHBsb3QoYWxwaGE9MC40KSArXG4gICAgICAgZ2VvbV9qaXR0ZXIod2lkdGg9MC4yKSArXG4gICAgICAgbGFicyh4PVxcU2V4XFwsIHk9XFxCTUkgKGtnL21eMilcXCwgdGl0bGU9XFxNYWxlIGFuZCBGZW1hbGUgQk1JXFwpXG5gYGBcbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
```r
ggplot(health,
       mapping = aes(x=sex, y=bmi)) +
       geom_boxplot(alpha=0.4) +
       geom_jitter(width=0.2) +
       labs(x=\Sex\, y=\BMI (kg/m^2)\, title=\Male and Female BMI\)
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->

/>

<!-- rnb-plot-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


**5.4 Bar chart — sample counts by hair color and sex**

Using `geom_bar()`, create a bar chart showing the number of samples for each
`hair_color`, filled by `sex`. Use `position = "dodge"` so the bars for each
sex are side by side.


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVaMmR3Ykc5MEtHaGxZV3gwYUN3Z2JXRndjR2x1WnlBOUlHRmxjeWg0UFdoaGFYSmZZMjlzYjNJcEtTQXJYRzRnSUdkbGIyMWZZbUZ5S0dGbGN5aGpiMnh2Y2oxelpYZ3BMQ0J3YjNOcGRHbHZiajFjSW1SdlpHZGxYQ0lwSUN0Y2JpQWdiR0ZpY3loNFBWd2lTR0ZwY2lCRGIyeHZjbHdpTENCMGFYUnNaVDFjSWtoaGFYSWdRMjlzYjNKY0lpd2dlVDFjSWtOdmRXNTBYQ0lwWEc1Z1lHQWlmUT09IC0tPlxuXG5gYGByXG5nZ3Bsb3QoaGVhbHRoLCBtYXBwaW5nID0gYWVzKHg9aGFpcl9jb2xvcikpICtcbiAgZ2VvbV9iYXIoYWVzKGNvbG9yPXNleCksIHBvc2l0aW9uPVxcZG9kZ2VcXCkgK1xuICBsYWJzKHg9XFxIYWlyIENvbG9yXFwsIHRpdGxlPVxcSGFpciBDb2xvclxcLCB5PVxcQ291bnRcXClcbmBgYFxuXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtZW5kIC0tPlxuIn0= -->

ggplot(health, mapping = aes(x=hair_color)) +
  geom_bar(aes(color=sex), position=\dodge\) +
  labs(x=\Hair Color\, title=\Hair Color\, y=\Count\)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dVoyZHdiRzkwS0dobFlXeDBhQ3dnYldGd2NHbHVaeUE5SUdGbGN5aDRQV2hoYVhKZlkyOXNiM0lwS1NBclhHNGdJR2RsYjIxZlltRnlLR0ZsY3loamIyeHZjajF6WlhncExDQndiM05wZEdsdmJqMWNYR1J2WkdkbFhGd3BJQ3RjYmlBZ2JHRmljeWg0UFZ4Y1NHRnBjaUJEYjJ4dmNseGNMQ0IwYVhSc1pUMWNYRWhoYVhJZ1EyOXNiM0pjWEN3Z2VUMWNYRU52ZFc1MFhGd3BYRzVnWUdCY2JtQmdZQ0o5IC0tPlxuXG5gYGByXG5gYGByXG5nZ3Bsb3QoaGVhbHRoLCBtYXBwaW5nID0gYWVzKHg9aGFpcl9jb2xvcikpICtcbiAgZ2VvbV9iYXIoYWVzKGNvbG9yPXNleCksIHBvc2l0aW9uPVxcZG9kZ2VcXCkgK1xuICBsYWJzKHg9XFxIYWlyIENvbG9yXFwsIHRpdGxlPVxcSGFpciBDb2xvclxcLCB5PVxcQ291bnRcXClcbmBgYFxuYGBgXG5cbjwhLS0gcm5iLXNvdXJjZS1lbmQgLS0+XG4ifQ== -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuZ2dwbG90KGhlYWx0aCwgbWFwcGluZyA9IGFlcyh4PWhhaXJfY29sb3IpKSArXG4gIGdlb21fYmFyKGFlcyhjb2xvcj1zZXgpLCBwb3NpdGlvbj1cXGRvZGdlXFwpICtcbiAgbGFicyh4PVxcSGFpciBDb2xvclxcLCB0aXRsZT1cXEhhaXIgQ29sb3JcXCwgeT1cXENvdW50XFwpXG5gYGBcbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
```r
ggplot(health, mapping = aes(x=hair_color)) +
  geom_bar(aes(color=sex), position=\dodge\) +
  labs(x=\Hair Color\, title=\Hair Color\, y=\Count\)
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

**5.5 Scatter plot — BMI vs. LDL**

Plot `bmi` (x) vs. `LDL_mgdl` (y), coloring points by `glucose_category`.
Choose a colorblind-friendly palette with `scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2")`.
Does there appear to be a relationship?


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVaMmR3Ykc5MEtHaGxZV3gwYUN3Z1hHNGdJQ0FnSUNBZ2JXRndjR2x1WnlBOUlHRmxjeWg0UFdKdGFTd2dlVDFNUkV4ZmJXZGtiQ2twSUN0Y2JpQWdJQ0FnSUNCblpXOXRYM0J2YVc1MEtHRmxjeWhqYjJ4dmRYSTlaMngxWTI5elpWOWpZWFJsWjI5eWVTa3BLeUFnSUNBZ1hHNGdJQ0FnSUNBZ2MyTmhiR1ZmWTI5c2IzSmZZbkpsZDJWeUtIQmhiR1YwZEdVOVhDSlRaWFF5WENJcElDdGNiaUFnSUNBZ0lDQnNZV0p6S0hnOVhDSkNUVWtnS0d0bkwyMWVNaWxjSWl3Z2VUMWNJa3hFVENBb2JXY3ZaRXdwWENJc0lIUnBkR3hsUFZ3aVFrMUpJR0Z1WkNCTVJFeGNJaWxjYm1CZ1lDSjkgLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmdncGxvdChoZWFsdGgsIFxuICAgICAgIG1hcHBpbmcgPSBhZXMoeD1ibWksIHk9TERMX21nZGwpKSArXG4gICAgICAgZ2VvbV9wb2ludChhZXMoY29sb3VyPWdsdWNvc2VfY2F0ZWdvcnkpKSsgICAgIFxuICAgICAgIHNjYWxlX2NvbG9yX2JyZXdlcihwYWxldHRlPVxcU2V0MlxcKSArXG4gICAgICAgbGFicyh4PVxcQk1JIChrZy9tXjIpXFwsIHk9XFxMREwgKG1nL2RMKVxcLCB0aXRsZT1cXEJNSSBhbmQgTERMXFwpXG5gYGBcblxuPCEtLSBybmItc291cmNlLWVuZCAtLT5cbiJ9 -->

ggplot(health, 
       mapping = aes(x=bmi, y=LDL_mgdl)) +
       geom_point(aes(colour=glucose_category))+     
       scale_color_brewer(palette=\Set2\) +
       labs(x=\BMI (kg/m^2)\, y=\LDL (mg/dL)\, title=\BMI and LDL\)

```r
ggplot(health, 
       mapping = aes(x=bmi, y=LDL_mgdl)) +
       geom_point(aes(colour=glucose_category))+     
       scale_color_brewer(palette=\Set2\) +
       labs(x=\BMI (kg/m^2)\, y=\LDL (mg/dL)\, title=\BMI and LDL\)
```
```

```r

# There appears to be no relationship between BMI and LDL levels.

<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


**5.6 Faceted plot — glucose vs. LDL by sex**

Plot `blood_glucose_mgdl` (x) vs. `LDL_mgdl` (y) as a scatter plot, then use
`facet_wrap(~ sex)` to create separate panels for Males and Females.


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVaMmR3Ykc5MEtHaGxZV3gwYUN3Z2JXRndjR2x1WnlBOUlHRmxjeWg0UFdKc2IyOWtYMmRzZFdOdmMyVmZiV2RrYkN3Z2VUMU1SRXhmYldka2JDa3BLMXh1SUNCblpXOXRYM0J2YVc1MEtHTnZiRzl5UFZ3aVlteDFaVndpS1N0Y2JpQWdabUZqWlhSZmQzSmhjQ2grYzJWNEtTQXJYRzRnSUd4aFluTW9lRDFjSWtKc2IyOWtJRWRzZFdOdmMyVWdLRzFuTDJSTUtWd2lMQ0I1UFZ3aVRFUk1JQ2h0Wnk5a1RDbGNJaXdnZEdsMGJHVTlYQ0pIYkhWamIzTmxJR0Z1WkNCTVJFd2dZbmtnVTJWNFhDSXBYRzVnWUdBaWZRPT0gLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmdncGxvdChoZWFsdGgsIG1hcHBpbmcgPSBhZXMoeD1ibG9vZF9nbHVjb3NlX21nZGwsIHk9TERMX21nZGwpKStcbiAgZ2VvbV9wb2ludChjb2xvcj1cXGJsdWVcXCkrXG4gIGZhY2V0X3dyYXAofnNleCkgK1xuICBsYWJzKHg9XFxCbG9vZCBHbHVjb3NlIChtZy9kTClcXCwgeT1cXExETCAobWcvZEwpXFwsIHRpdGxlPVxcR2x1Y29zZSBhbmQgTERMIGJ5IFNleFxcKVxuYGBgXG5cbjwhLS0gcm5iLXNvdXJjZS1lbmQgLS0+XG4ifQ== -->

ggplot(health, mapping = aes(x=blood_glucose_mgdl, y=LDL_mgdl))+
  geom_point(color=\blue\)+
  facet_wrap(~sex) +
  labs(x=\Blood Glucose (mg/dL)\, y=\LDL (mg/dL)\, title=\Glucose and LDL by Sex\)



<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dVoyZHdiRzkwS0dobFlXeDBhQ3dnYldGd2NHbHVaeUE5SUdGbGN5aDRQV0pzYjI5a1gyZHNkV052YzJWZmJXZGtiQ3dnZVQxTVJFeGZiV2RrYkNrcEsxeHVJQ0JuWlc5dFgzQnZhVzUwS0dOdmJHOXlQVnhjWW14MVpWeGNLU3RjYmlBZ1ptRmpaWFJmZDNKaGNDaCtjMlY0S1NBclhHNGdJR3hoWW5Nb2VEMWNYRUpzYjI5a0lFZHNkV052YzJVZ0tHMW5MMlJNS1Z4Y0xDQjVQVnhjVEVSTUlDaHRaeTlrVENsY1hDd2dkR2wwYkdVOVhGeEhiSFZqYjNObElHRnVaQ0JNUkV3Z1lua2dVMlY0WEZ3cFhHNWdZR0JjYm1CZ1lDSjkgLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmBgYHJcbmdncGxvdChoZWFsdGgsIG1hcHBpbmcgPSBhZXMoeD1ibG9vZF9nbHVjb3NlX21nZGwsIHk9TERMX21nZGwpKStcbiAgZ2VvbV9wb2ludChjb2xvcj1cXGJsdWVcXCkrXG4gIGZhY2V0X3dyYXAofnNleCkgK1xuICBsYWJzKHg9XFxCbG9vZCBHbHVjb3NlIChtZy9kTClcXCwgeT1cXExETCAobWcvZEwpXFwsIHRpdGxlPVxcR2x1Y29zZSBhbmQgTERMIGJ5IFNleFxcKVxuYGBgXG5gYGBcblxuPCEtLSBybmItc291cmNlLWVuZCAtLT5cbiJ9 -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuZ2dwbG90KGhlYWx0aCwgbWFwcGluZyA9IGFlcyh4PWJsb29kX2dsdWNvc2VfbWdkbCwgeT1MRExfbWdkbCkpK1xuICBnZW9tX3BvaW50KGNvbG9yPVxcYmx1ZVxcKStcbiAgZmFjZXRfd3JhcCh+c2V4KSArXG4gIGxhYnMoeD1cXEJsb29kIEdsdWNvc2UgKG1nL2RMKVxcLCB5PVxcTERMIChtZy9kTClcXCwgdGl0bGU9XFxHbHVjb3NlIGFuZCBMREwgYnkgU2V4XFwpXG5gYGBcbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
```r
ggplot(health, mapping = aes(x=blood_glucose_mgdl, y=LDL_mgdl))+
  geom_point(color=\blue\)+
  facet_wrap(~sex) +
  labs(x=\Blood Glucose (mg/dL)\, y=\LDL (mg/dL)\, title=\Glucose and LDL by Sex\)
```
```

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

---

## Part 6 — Putting it together (open-ended)

**6.1** Using any combination of `dplyr` and `ggplot2`, create one additional
plot of your choice that reveals something interesting about this dataset.
Write 2–3 sentences below your plot describing what you observe.


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

ggplot(health, 
   mapping = aes(x=eye_color, y=bmi)) +
   geom_boxplot(aes(color=sex))+
   labs(x=\Eye Color\, y=\BMI (kg/m^2)\, title=\Eye Color and BMI\)

````

```r
ggplot(health, 
   mapping = aes(x=eye_color, y=bmi)) +
   geom_boxplot(aes(color=sex))+
   labs(x=\Eye Color\, y=\BMI (kg/m^2)\, title=\Eye Color and BMI\)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1wbG90LWJlZ2luIGV5Sm9aV2xuYUhRaU9qTXdPUzR3TWpNMUxDSjNhV1IwYUNJNk5UQXdMQ0prY0draU9pMHhMQ0p6YVhwbFgySmxhR0YyYVc5eUlqb3dMQ0pqYjI1a2FYUnBiMjV6SWpwYlhYMD0gLS0+XG5cbjxpbWcgc3JjPVxcZGF0YTppbWFnZS9wbmc7YmFzZTY0XG4ifQ== -->

<img src=:image/png;base64 ```

Observations

I thought it would be interesting to look at the relationship between eye color and BMI organized by sex. Unexpectedly, there appears to be no statistical difference between the BMI for those with different eye colors, nor is there a difference between the median BMI between males and females with the same eye color. Interestingly, males with gray eyes have no variance in their BMI’ and this is because there is only one male in the sample with gray eyes, resulting in a flat line representing the one occurrence. Males and females with hazel eyes seem to have the greatest difference in mean BMI, a relationship that likely cannot be explained by and enforced with definitive evidence.
